tokens: Okay, Ken, if you'll give us Low Altitude on the V-over-H Override.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You can go to Center/Off position on the V-over-H Override, and you can go Auto on the High Gain.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, V-over-H Override is Center/Off and the High Gain is in (Pause) Auto.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Just for your info, the field geology team are in the final phase of making their plans for EVA-2 tomorrow.
So, if you've got any additional comments, why, better make them this pass because they'll (have to) have it all firmed up.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Oh, I think they're smarter than I am.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Don't get too carried away (with modesty) up there.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: (Laughter) No, I would really - I'd really hope they make it to North Ray, though.
I think that's going to be different than we expected it to be.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: (Long Pause) Okay, Ken, we are ready for Pan Camera Power to Off at this time.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, Pan Camera Power is coming Off.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: How did we make out in our discussion on North Ray low altitude there as far as being able to see the white albedo or not?
Or, were you too far to the south?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: It depends which rev you want to talk about.
On...When I looked at her on the first day, right after DOI, there wasn't any...There was two craters, but no rays.
When I went back and looked at him on landing morning, there's a slight ray.
But North Ray still doesn't stand out as being the bright guy that South Ray does.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, it's obvious I was talking about the landing day.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: (Laughter) I gathered that it would be the case.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Right now I would agree with you on anything, Stu.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Yeah, I ought to...I'll be quiet here and let you concentrate on your eating.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I got...I got the Modern Jazz Quartet playing "Porgy and Bess," and I got orange/grapefruit - some of it in the bag, some of it on the bulkheads - (and) potato soup.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: With all of that, now while ago you talked about some Casperellos - with all that Beta Candle and all that food, you don't have any such thing as a Casperette, do you?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: (Laughter) No, I'm afraid I left that at home.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's about all this place is lacking, though.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, Ken, just another comment to close the loop on you on that P23s, the...Like I said before, the marking data was very good, and it came up with an horizon of 33 and loaded is a value of 28.
And, after massaging all the data, decided to not change it since we've shown on the other missions that coming back...have a tendency to have a lower horizon.
So, looks like we're just swinging with what we got there.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Couldn't pass up a chance to watch the landing site one more time, and so I took a quick break from chow and went and watched it.
And had a couple of questions in mind and only got two of them answered.
One of them is that...Counting the layers in North and South Ray, and South Ray looks different than North in that South Ray shows three distinct light and dark sequences.
I suspect they're slumps, but there's at least three bands.
North Ray doesn't have any of that sort of thing.

I took another look for the terraces and the whole area...The thing that we thought looked so distinctly different in the photographs, (that it) looked like Stone Mountain and Smoky Mountain were two different things and something came in the middle of it; and it doesn't look that way to me at all today.
It looks to me like it's really all...Almost all part of the same material.
And I've drawn another little mark on my map.
It's just about where you folks said you thought the LM was, except a little farther over to the north.
It turns out that there's one little bright speckle there that doesn't look like craters.
I don't see anything except the speckle.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Now, go straight north of the - of the LM and a little to the west, there's three small craters there that are covered with what looks like...(covered) by a ray, now.
Where are you talking from that?
Did you fade out, or did you stop talking?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I had you off of Vox and I forgot to push the key down, I had gotten so use to it.
No, I gave you an 80 and about - oh, you were lookin in the right area.
And, let me see what I can give you for coordinates on that.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, and you know I'm not overhead long enough to be sure that that's what I'm looking at, but it looked to me like it had a different kind of glint to it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: By the time I got the camera, it was gone.
But that little buildup that we talked that was just to the west of Lassell, I had it spotted, and I was grabbing for the camera, and I couldn't find it again after I got back.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, Ken, if you want to go Accept, we'll uplink the jet monitor load; and, it's your choice whether you want us to initiate it or you want to initiate it.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: All right, you guys can do that.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Go to Block, and the EMP is running.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Be with you in a second.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're showing a little over 14 minutes to LOS; but, we're going to lose data before that.
And, we'd like to get that E-memory dump just any time you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And, Ken, on the High Gain, we'd like Reacq(uisition).
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, you have Reacq; Pitch, zero; Yaw, 170.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Ken, we're going to lose comm with you here shortly.
And, we'd like to get your onboard readouts and if you would get your book to copy, we've got a TEI-41 pad.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, I'll give you some readouts first.
Battery C is 36.7; battery B, 36.3; battery A, 36.8.
I guess all you really needed out of that was battery C though.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, you'd like to have...(Hearing Stu) You want the quantities?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, Ken; let's go Auto now with the High Gain.
We're going to lose you in Reacq.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We've got all of those and I'd like to give you a TEI-41 pad.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, it's TEI-41: SPS/G&N, 38709; plus 0.72, plus 1.33; 155:06:58.45; plus 3355.2; plus 1151.0; minus 0235.0; 181, 095, 020; rest of the pad NA.
The GDC align - same as circ.
Longitude at T: plus 173.29, assumes no LOPC-1.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Sirius and Rigel with the same numbers for circ; two jets, 17 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's a good readhack and we'd like to bid you good night and remind you that your Logic Power for the SIM bay is still on.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I'll see you at the mare.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And we'd like to have Reacq at LOS.
And that will be Pitch, zero; Yaw, 170...
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I have Pitch, zero; and 170 set in.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly
